input.prompt:
My .NET MAUI page shows blank labels even though my ViewModel properties
have values. Here is my XAML:
```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml">
    <VerticalStackLayout>
        <Label Text="{Binding Title}" />
        <Label Text="{Binding Description}" />
    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```
What am I doing wrong?

input.initialPrompt:
My .NET MAUI page shows blank labels even though my ViewModel properties
have values. Here is my XAML:
```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml">
    <VerticalStackLayout>
        <Label Text="{Binding Title}" />
        <Label Text="{Binding Description}" />
    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```
What am I doing wrong?

data.content:
You're missing the `BindingContext`. The page doesn't know which object to bind against. Set it in the code-behind or XAML:

**Option 1 – Code-behind (constructor):**
```csharp
public MyPage()
{
    InitializeComponent();
    BindingContext = new MyViewModel();
}
```

**Option 2 – XAML:**
```xml
<ContentPage xmlns:vm="clr-namespace:YourApp.ViewModels"
             ...>
    <ContentPage.BindingContext>
        <vm:MyViewModel />
    </ContentPage.BindingContext>
    ...
</ContentPage>
```

Also ensure your ViewModel implements `INotifyPropertyChanged` (or inherits from `ObservableObject` in the MVVM Toolkit) so the UI updates when property values change.
data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a common .NET MAUI data binding issue. Their XAML looks correct for binding syntax, but the most common reason labels appear blank despite ViewModel properties having values is that the `BindingContext` isn't set.

I don't need to use any tools for this - it's a straightforward knowledge question about .NET MAUI data binding.

The issue is that they haven't set the `BindingContext` of the page to their ViewModel instance. Without it, the bindings have nothing to resolve against.

Additionally, if properties change after initial binding, the ViewModel needs to implement `INotifyPropertyChanged`.
